mnemonics.title: Euler's Formula
mnemonics.content: V - E + F = 2: Remember 'Very Evil Fairies' or imagine a cube: 8 vertices - 12 edges + 6 faces = 2 (think of a die showing 2 on top)

mnemonics.title: Planar Edge Limit
mnemonics.content: For planar: 3n - 6 edges maximum. Think 'Planar = 3 sides per face, multiply by n, subtract the extras' - every face needs 3 edges, counted twice gives 3f ≤ 2e

mnemonics.title: Kuratowski's Non-Planar Graphs
mnemonics.content: K5 and K3,3 are the 'Criminals' - remember them as 'K- criminals': K5 has 5 vertices, K3,3 has 3+3=6 vertices. No subdivisions allowed!

mnemonics.title: Four Color Theorem
mnemonics.content: MAPS need 4 colors: Imagine coloring a map of countries - never need more than 4 colors to ensure no adjacent countries share a color

acronyms.usage: Euler's formula for connected planar graphs - the VEF equation always equals 2!
The two fundamental non-planar graphs in Kuratowski's theorem - subdivisions of these make graphs non-planar

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a convex polyhedron like a cube or tetrahedron. Count vertices (V), edges (E), and faces (F). They always satisfy V - E + F = 2. This is the sphere topology!
Draw a pentagon with all diagonals - you'll always have at least one crossing. Try to redraw it multiple ways - it never works without crossings.
Draw two rows of 3 dots each. Connect every top dot to every bottom dot. You'll see it always crosses - this is the utility graph! Impossible to draw without crossings.
Think of graph isomorphism like rearranging furniture in a room. The room (graph structure) stays the same, just the positions (labels) change. If you can match all relationships, they're isomorphic!

analogies.analogy: Two puzzles with the same pieces arranged differently are isomorphic - same pieces, same connections, just labeled differently. It's like checking if two friendship circles have the same friendship patterns.

Think of a subway map - if you can draw all train lines without any crossing, it's planar. The London Tube map is planar, but you couldn't add a direct line from any station to any other without crossings in most cases.

Homeomorphism is like different shaped pizza slices cut from the same original - same topology, just stretched or subdivided. A circle, oval, and square are all homeomorphic (can be deformed into each other).
